_sources.discrepancies: Minnesota has no photo ID requirement — voters sign the poll book (vouching system also available). NCSL categorizes MN as 'no document required'. First-time voters who register on Election Day must present proof of residence; this is not a photo ID requirement per se. Minnesota's same-day registration is a long-standing feature. Primary is set by statute as the second Tuesday in August.

earlyVoting.notes: Early (absentee in-person) voting available starting 46 days before the primary at county auditor or city clerk offices. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early in-person absentee voting starts 46 days before the general election. Sites and hours vary by county/city. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

Early in-person absentee voting begins 46 days before the general election and is available at county auditor and city/township clerk offices. Same-day registration available through Election Day. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Minnesota does not prohibit phones at polling places. Voters may not take photos of their marked ballot. No electioneering within 100 feet of the polling place entrance.
additionalRules: No photo ID required — voters sign the poll book. A registered voter may also vouch for an unregistered neighbor (vouching system).
Open primary: voters pick any party's primary ballot without declaring affiliation.
Same-day voter registration available on Election Day at your polling place.
First-time voters registering on Election Day must present proof of residence (utility bill, bank statement, lease, etc.).
No runoffs in Minnesota — the candidate with the most votes wins.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 1 day before the election (received by the county auditor or city clerk) for mailed application; in-person absentee voting available up through Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county auditor by 3 p.m. on Election Day if returned by mail; or deposited in drop box by 8 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Mail it back so it arrives by 3 p.m. Election Day, or drop it in a secure drop box by 8 p.m. Election Day.
